hypothesis: If language models for materials design are fine-tuned using Direct Preference Optimization guided by physics-based thermodynamic simulation feedback, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy compositions with higher reward scores, greater novelty, and better coverage of the valid compositional space compared to supervised fine-tuning alone, few-shot prompting of frontier models, and agentic baselines, because preference learning directly optimizes the model's generative distribution toward compositions satisfying hierarchical thermodynamic synthesis objectives rather than relying solely on pattern replication from known examples.
